title: Deep Breathing & Psychological Wellbeing Survey
description: This survey assesses your psychological wellbeing through breathing habits, task confidence, and mental clarity.

questions.text: How often do you take slow, deep breaths before starting a difficult task?
How confident do you feel when beginning a new academic or work assignment?
How often do you feel a sense of tightness or constriction in your chest during stressful moments?
How easily can you clear your mind and focus when you sit down to work?
How frequently do you pause and breathe mindfully when feeling overwhelmed?
How often do you experience shallow or rapid breathing when under pressure?
How calm do you feel internally when facing an unexpected challenge?
How often do you use breathing exercises as part of a daily routine?
How much does anxiety or nervousness interfere with your ability to start tasks?
How well do you recover psychologically after making a mistake or failing at a task?

options.label: Very well — I move on quickly
I dwell on it for a long time

questions.text: How often do you feel mentally exhausted before your day even begins?
How aware are you of your own breathing patterns during stressful situations?
How often do you feel a sense of dread or avoidance when approaching an important task?
How would you describe your overall sense of inner peace on a typical day?
How often do you feel physically tense (shoulders, jaw, hands) when working?
How frequently do you complete tasks without procrastinating?
How well do you sleep the night before an important event or deadline?
How often do you use self-talk or internal encouragement before a challenging task?
How often do you feel that your mind goes blank when you need to perform under pressure?
How satisfied are you with your overall psychological wellbeing right now?

states.label: Excellent Psychological Wellbeing
states.description: You demonstrate outstanding psychological balance. Your breathing awareness and task confidence are at an optimal level. No intervention required.

states.label: Good Psychological Wellbeing
states.description: You are in a good psychological state with minor areas to strengthen. Continue your healthy habits and consider exploring mindfulness techniques.

states.label: Mild Psychological Tension
states.description: You experience moderate tension that occasionally affects your confidence and focus. Practising daily deep-breathing routines may help significantly.

states.label: Moderate Psychological Stress
states.description: Noticeable stress and anxiety are affecting your daily functioning. Consider structured breathing exercises, journaling, or speaking with a counsellor.

states.label: Elevated Psychological Distress
states.description: You are experiencing elevated distress that is likely interfering with your academic or work performance. Seeking psychological support is advisable.

states.label: High Psychological Distress
states.description: Your results indicate significant psychological difficulty. Professional psychological support is strongly recommended.

states.label: Severe Psychological Crisis
states.description: You are in a severe state of psychological distress. Please reach out to a mental health professional or trusted person immediately.
